metadata.title: DNG Camera Profile (DCP) Parser
metadata.formatDiscovery: CRITICAL FORMAT PIVOT: Real Adobe DCP files use BINARY TIFF tags (50700-52600), NOT XML in tag 50740. Complete rewrite performed. See docs/stories/9-1-dcp-parser-FORMAT-PIVOT.md for details.

story.iWant: Recipe to parse DNG Camera Profile (.dcp) files and extract tone curve adjustments
story.soThat: I can convert Adobe camera profiles to other preset formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate, .costyle) and use them across different editing software

tasks.task: Set Up DCP Package Structure (AC: All) - Create internal/formats/dcp/ directory with types.go (DNG tag constants, binary structs), parse.go, tiff.go (DNG version conversion, binary extractors), profile.go (binary tone curve analysis), parse_test.go, and use existing testdata/dcp/ (36 real DCP files)
Add github.com/google/tiff Dependency (AC-1) - Add dependency to go.mod and verify imports
Implement TIFF/DNG Reading with Version Conversion (AC-1) - Implement readTIFF() with DNG version conversion (IIRC/MMCR → version 42), validate TIFF/DNG magic bytes

Implement Binary Tag Extractors (AC-2, AC-3, AC-4) - Implement extractProfileName() (tag 52552, OPTIONAL), extractToneCurve() (tag 50940 as float32 pairs), extractColorMatrix() (tags 50721-50722 as SRational[9]), extractBaselineExposure() (tag 50730)
Analyze Binary Tone Curve Shape (AC-3) - Implement analyzeToneCurve() to extract exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows from 0.0-1.0 normalized float32 curve points
Implement Parse() Function (AC-5) - Orchestrate TIFF/DNG reading → binary tag extraction → tone curve analysis → UniversalRecipe mapping
Error Handling (AC-6) - Validate TIFF/DNG magic bytes, handle DNG version conversion, wrap errors with context, handle corrupt files gracefully, clamp out-of-range values
Use Existing DCP Sample Files (AC-7) - Use 36 real Adobe DCP files from testdata/dcp/ (Nikon, Hasselblad, Leica camera profiles)

Write Unit Tests (AC-7) - Test Parse() with valid DCP (3+ samples), missing ProfileName (tag 52552 optional), missing tone curve, non-identity matrices, corrupt TIFF, tone curve analysis edge cases with 0.0-1.0 float values. Coverage: 63.3% total (parse.go: 76%, profile.go: 90%+)
Documentation (AC-1 to AC-6) - Add package comments describing binary DNG format, function documentation, create FORMAT-PIVOT.md documenting format discovery, update story 9-1 markdown

criterion.title: Parse Binary DNG Structure
criterion.item: ✅ Read DNG file using github.com/google/tiff library
✅ Validate TIFF/DNG magic bytes (II/MM for TIFF, IIRC/MMCR for DNG)
✅ Convert DNG version bytes (IIRC/MMCR → version 42) for google/tiff compatibility
✅ Parse TIFF Image File Directory (IFD) structure
✅ Extract binary DNG tags (50700-52600 range)
✅ Handle DNG version conversion for google/tiff compatibility
✅ Report parsing errors with clear messages

criterion.title: Extract Binary Profile Data
criterion.item: ✅ Extract tag 52552 (ProfileName) - OPTIONAL field (empty string if missing)
✅ Extract tag 50940 (ToneCurve) as binary float32 array
✅ Extract tags 50721-50722 (ColorMatrix1/2) as SRational arrays
✅ Extract tag 50730 (BaselineExposureOffset) as SRational
✅ Handle missing optional tags gracefully (return empty/nil)
✅ Support DNG 1.0-1.6 binary format

criterion.title: Parse Binary Tone Curve
criterion.item: ✅ Parse tag 50940 as array of float32 pairs (input, output)
✅ Extract tone curve points normalized to 0.0-1.0 range (not 0-255)

✅ Analyze tone curve shape to extract parameters: Exposure (midpoint shift 0.5 → X, normalized by 0.25), Contrast (slope difference top 0.75 - bottom 0.25), Highlights (top-end curve shape point 1.0 deviation), Shadows (bottom-end curve shape point 0.0 deviation)
✅ Handle missing tone curve (return zeros - linear curve)
✅ Clamp extracted values to valid UniversalRecipe ranges

criterion.title: Parse Binary Color Matrices
criterion.item: ✅ Parse tags 50721-50722 as 9 SRational values each
✅ Convert SRational (numerator/denominator) to float64
✅ Recognize identity matrices (diagonal 1.0, off-diagonal 0.0)
✅ Log warning if non-identity matrices detected
✅ Store matrix data in Metadata map for future use

criterion.title: Return UniversalRecipe
criterion.item: ✅ Map binary tone curve to UniversalRecipe fields
✅ Preserve profile metadata (name from tag 52552 or empty string)
✅ Store baseline exposure offset in metadata
✅ Return populated *models.UniversalRecipe and nil error

criterion.title: Handle Binary Parsing Errors
criterion.item: ✅ Validate TIFF/DNG magic bytes before parsing
✅ Handle DNG version conversion errors
✅ Handle corrupt TIFF files gracefully (no panics)
✅ Handle invalid binary data (wrong lengths, zero denominators)
✅ Handle missing required tags (currently all tags are optional)

criterion.title: Unit Test Coverage
criterion.item: ✅ Tests with 3+ real Adobe DCP samples (tested 36 files)
✅ Test edge cases (missing ProfileName, missing ToneCurve)
✅ Test coverage: 63.3% total (parse.go: 76%, profile.go: 90%+)
✅ All tests pass

doc.title: Product Requirements Document - Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Parse DCP (DNG Camera Profile) files per Adobe DNG Specification 1.6, generate DCP files from UniversalRecipe color adjustments, support BINARY DNG tag structure (tags 50700-52600), validate compatibility with Adobe Camera Raw and Lightroom

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Architecture components: internal/formats/dcp/ package with parse.go (TIFF/DNG reading → binary tag extraction), tiff.go (low-level TIFF tag operations using github.com/google/tiff, DNG version conversion, binary tag extractors for tags 50940, 50721-50722, 52552, 50730), profile.go (binary tone curve analysis with 0.0-1.0 normalized values), types.go (DNG tag constants, binary structs). Key design: DNG version conversion (IIRC/MMCR → version 42), binary tag extraction, use identity matrices for non-calibration use cases.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: DCP Camera Profile Support
doc.section: Detailed Design - DCP File Structure and Tone Curve Analysis
doc.snippet: CRITICAL FORMAT DISCOVERY: DCP files use BINARY TIFF tags (50700-52600), NOT XML in tag 50740. Tag 50940 stores tone curve as float32 pairs (0.0-1.0 normalized). Tag 52552 stores ProfileName (OPTIONAL). Focus on tone curve adjustments (exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows) mapped to UniversalRecipe. Identity color matrices for non-calibration use. Tone curve analysis: midpoint shift (0.5 → X) → Exposure, curve slope (0.75 - 0.25) → Contrast, top-end (1.0) → Highlights, bottom-end (0.0) → Shadows. DNG version conversion required (IIRC/MMCR → version 42 for google/tiff compatibility).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: DCP Camera Profile Support
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts - Go Struct Mapping
doc.snippet: CameraProfile struct with ToneCurve []ToneCurvePoint, ColorMatrix1/2 *Matrix. ToneCurvePoint contains Input/Output float64 (0.0-1.0 normalized range). Matrix struct with [3][3]float64 for color transformation. Binary format: tone curves are float32 pairs (8 bytes per point), color matrices are 9 SRational values (72 bytes total, 8 bytes per SRational = int32 numerator + int32 denominator), ProfileName is ASCII string (OPTIONAL).

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: DCP Camera Profile Support
doc.section: APIs and Interfaces - Parse and Generate Functions
doc.snippet: Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error): Read TIFF/DNG (with version conversion) → extract binary tags (52552, 50940, 50721-50722, 50730) → analyze tone curve → return UniversalRecipe. Key helpers: readTIFF() (DNG version conversion), extractProfileName() (tag 52552, optional), extractToneCurve() (tag 50940, float32 pairs), extractColorMatrix() (tags 50721-50722, SRational[9]), extractBaselineExposure() (tag 50730), analyzeToneCurve() (0.0-1.0 normalized), profileToUniversal(). Error handling with fmt.Errorf wrapping.

doc.title: Format Pivot Documentation
doc.snippet: Original assumption: DCP files contain XML in tag 50740. Reality: Real Adobe DCP files use binary TIFF tags (50700-52600). Complete rewrite required. Tag 52552 (ProfileName) is OPTIONAL. Tone curves are float32 pairs (0.0-1.0), not 0-255 integers. Color matrices are SRational arrays, not XML elements. DNG version requires conversion ("IIRC"/"MMCR" → version 42). Impact on story 9-2: Must generate binary tags, not XML.

artifact.reason: Reference pattern for Parse() function signature, error handling with fmt.Errorf wrapping, validation flow (validateFileStructure → extractParameters → validateParameters → buildRecipe)
Reference pattern for XML parsing (not used in DCP binary format but useful for error handling), ConversionError wrapping, validate → unmarshal → extract → validate → build flow
Reference pattern for parameter extraction and validation, ConversionError usage, buildRecipe construction without builder pattern
Most recent parser implementation (Story 8-1), shows current parsing patterns, metadata handling, parameter mapping with clamping

Complete UniversalRecipe struct definition showing all available fields for tone curve mapping (Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Blacks, Whites), Metadata map for unsupported DCP data (profile name, baseline exposure, color matrices)
Shows format detection and Parse() integration pattern for extending converter with new DCP format (Story 9-5 will integrate here)
Format detection logic pattern for adding DCP recognition (TIFF/DNG magic bytes II/MM or IIRC/MMCR + .dcp extension check)

go.reason: NEW DEPENDENCY - Complete TIFF library for reading/writing binary TIFF tags (tags 50700-52600). DNG version conversion required (IIRC/MMCR → version 42). Go stdlib image/tiff is decoder-only and doesn't support custom tags.
Binary data conversion: uint32 to float32 (tone curves), int32 numerator/denominator to float64 (SRational color matrices)
Pointer conversion for uint32 bits to float32 values (tone curve extraction)
String operations for ProfileName null termination trimming
Error wrapping with fmt.Errorf and %w verb
TIFF data stream handling with bytes.NewReader, DNG version conversion (copy modifiedData)
Math operations for tone curve analysis (interpolation, clamping, math.Abs for float comparisons)

constraints.constraint: Follow exact pattern of existing format packages (np3, xmp, lrtemplate, costyle): Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error) signature
Maintain hub-and-spoke architecture: DCP → UniversalRecipe only (no direct format-to-format conversion)
Performance target: <200ms DCP parsing (slower than other formats acceptable due to TIFF overhead and binary tag extraction)
Test coverage: ≥85% target (achieved 63.3% total with parse.go: 76%, profile.go: 90%+; lower total due to untested generate.go which is story 9-2 scope)
Error handling: Use fmt.Errorf with %w verb for error wrapping, no panics, all errors via return value
Validation: Fail-fast approach - validate TIFF/DNG magic bytes before parsing, convert DNG version if needed, handle missing optional tags gracefully
Focus on tone curves only: Skip full camera calibration (ForwardMatrix, ColorMatrix, dual illuminant) for MVP
Use identity color matrices: Diagonal 1.0, off-diagonal 0.0 (no camera-specific calibration)
Clamp extracted values: Exposure (-2.0 to +2.0), Contrast/Highlights/Shadows (-1.0 to +1.0 normalized, converted to -100 to +100 int for UniversalRecipe)
Handle optional ProfileName: Tag 52552 is OPTIONAL in many DCP files - return empty string if missing, not an error
Binary format normalization: Tone curves use 0.0-1.0 float range, not 0-255 int range
DNG version conversion: IIRC (0x49494352) or MMCR (0x4D4D4352) must be converted to version 42 (0x002A) for google/tiff compatibility

interface.name: Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error)
interface.description: Main DCP parser function - validates TIFF/DNG structure with version conversion, extracts binary tags (52552, 50940, 50721-50722, 50730), analyzes tone curve with 0.0-1.0 normalization, returns UniversalRecipe or error

interface.name: readTIFF(data []byte) (*tiff.TIFF, error)
interface.description: Reads TIFF/DNG structure using github.com/google/tiff, validates magic bytes (II/MM for TIFF, IIRC/MMCR for DNG), converts DNG version to version 42 if needed, returns parsed TIFF or error

interface.name: extractProfileName(ifd tiff.IFD) (string, error)
interface.description: Extracts ASCII string from TIFF tag 52552 (ProfileName tag), returns empty string if tag not present (OPTIONAL field), trims null termination

interface.name: extractToneCurve(ifd tiff.IFD) ([]ToneCurvePoint, error)
interface.description: Extracts binary float32 array from TIFF tag 50940 (ProfileToneCurve), each point is 2 float32 values (input, output) = 8 bytes, returns ToneCurvePoint array with 0.0-1.0 normalized values

interface.name: extractColorMatrix(ifd tiff.IFD, tag int) (*Matrix, error)
interface.signature: func extractColorMatrix(ifd tiff.IFD, tag int) (*Matrix, error)
interface.description: Extracts 9 SRational values from TIFF tags 50721/50722 (ColorMatrix1/2), each SRational is 8 bytes (int32 numerator + int32 denominator), converts to 3x3 float64 matrix, validates zero denominators

interface.name: extractBaselineExposure(ifd tiff.IFD) (float64, error)
interface.description: Extracts SRational value from TIFF tag 50730 (BaselineExposureOffset), converts int32 numerator/denominator to float64

interface.name: analyzeToneCurve(points []ToneCurvePoint) (exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows float64)
interface.signature: func analyzeToneCurve(points []ToneCurvePoint) (exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows float64)
interface.description: Analyzes binary tone curve shape (0.0-1.0 normalized) to extract UniversalRecipe parameters: midpoint shift (0.5 → X, normalized by 0.25) → Exposure, slope (top 0.75 - bottom 0.25) → Contrast, top-end (1.0) → Highlights, bottom-end (0.0) → Shadows

interface.name: profileToUniversal(profile *CameraProfile) *models.UniversalRecipe
interface.description: Converts parsed DCP CameraProfile to UniversalRecipe, calls analyzeToneCurve(), adds baseline exposure offset, stores profile name in metadata (even if empty), logs warning if non-identity matrices detected

interface.name: CameraProfile struct
interface.signature: type CameraProfile struct { ProfileName string; ToneCurve []ToneCurvePoint; ColorMatrix1/2 *Matrix; BaselineExposure float64 }
interface.description: Represents parsed DNG Camera Profile binary data, includes tone curve points (0.0-1.0 normalized), color matrices (3x3 float64), baseline exposure offset, ProfileName (may be empty if tag 52552 not present)

interface.name: ToneCurvePoint struct
interface.signature: type ToneCurvePoint struct { Input float64; Output float64 } // 0.0-1.0 normalized
interface.description: Single point on piecewise linear tone curve, binary format uses float32 pairs (8 bytes per point), converted to float64 for processing, normalized to 0.0-1.0 range

interface.name: Matrix struct
interface.description: 3x3 color calibration matrix, binary format stores 9 SRational values (72 bytes total), converted to float64 array

tests.standards: Unit tests follow Recipe's established patterns: table-driven tests with real sample files in testdata/ directory, descriptive test names with Test{Function}_{Scenario} format, assertion of both success cases and error conditions, coverage measured with go test -cover. DCP tests use 36 real Adobe DCP files (Nikon, Hasselblad, Leica camera profiles) from testdata/dcp/. Tests verify binary format parsing with 0.0-1.0 float values, optional ProfileName handling (tag 52552), DNG version conversion (IIRC/MMCR → version 42), SRational extraction, float32 binary conversion. Test organization: parse_test.go (Parse function tests with real DCP samples, edge cases, error handling, tone curve analysis algorithms). Coverage: 63.3% total (parse.go: 76%, profile.go: 90%+), lower total due to untested generate.go (story 9-2 scope).

ideas.idea: TestParse_ValidDCP - Parse 3 real Adobe DCP samples (Nikon Z f Standard/Portrait, Hasselblad X1D-50), verify UniversalRecipe fields populated correctly, verify DNG version conversion successful
TestReadTIFF_ValidFile - Verify TIFF/DNG structure parsed correctly, IFD accessible, magic bytes validated, DNG version converted to 42
TestParse_CorruptTIFF - Empty file, invalid magic bytes, truncated file should return error without panic
TestExtractProfileName_Present - Tag 52552 exists and returns ASCII string, null termination trimmed
TestExtractProfileName_Missing - Tag 52552 missing returns empty string (not error, OPTIONAL field)
TestExtractToneCurve_ValidData - Tag 50940 float32 array extracts correctly, points normalized to 0.0-1.0 range
TestExtractColorMatrix_ValidData - Tags 50721/50722 SRational[9] extract correctly, 3x3 matrix conversion, zero denominator handling
TestAnalyzeToneCurve_LinearCurve - Linear curve (0.0,0.0 → 1.0,1.0) returns zero exposure/contrast/highlights/shadows
TestAnalyzeToneCurve_ExposureShift - Midpoint shift (0.5 → 0.625) extracts positive exposure value (+0.5 expected)
TestAnalyzeToneCurve_ContrastAdjustment - Steeper slope (0.125 at 0.25, 0.878 at 0.75) extracts positive contrast value (+0.5 expected)
TestFindPoint - Exact match and interpolation logic with 0.0-1.0 float values
TestIsIdentityMatrix - Identity matrix detected (diagonal 1.0, off-diagonal 0.0 with ±0.001 tolerance)
TestIsIdentityMatrix_NonIdentity - Non-identity matrix detected (1.5 on diagonal, 0.1 off-diagonal)
TestParse_MetadataPreservation - ProfileName stored in recipe.Metadata["profile_name"], baseline exposure stored, color matrices stored if non-identity
TestClampFloat64 - Value clamping within range, below min, above max
TestClampInt - Integer clamping for Contrast/Highlights/Shadows conversion
All tests pass with 36 real DCP files, coverage 63.3% total (parse.go: 76%, profile.go: 90%+)
